summary: High‑energy 1969 late show with blistering Other One and Alligator suite, captured on a decent soundboard tape despite cassette hiss.
review: The performance is hailed as one of the finest examples of ’69 Dead, featuring soaring guitar work on The Other One, a monster Cryptical>Other One sequence, and an all‑out Alligator → Drums → Jam marathon that even the Feedback section gets rave reviews.  The soundboard source sounds good overall but suffers from cassette‑master hiss, heavy compression and fade‑ins/outs, which keeps the technical quality at a solid‑good level.

ai_rating.rationale: Over ten detailed reviews overwhelmingly praise the musical performance while noting consistent but non‑deal recording issues; weighting show quality (70%) and recording quality (30%) yields a high overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Cassette master source introduces noticeable hiss and heavy compression that makes EQ difficult; fade‑ins/outs are present but the mix remains decent and listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers describe a high‑energy first set that builds into an explosive second set; jams are fully realized, with the Alligator suite and extended drum/feedback sections highlighted as peak moments.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar is repeatedly called acid‑drenched, soaring, full of tension and pyrotechnic brilliance throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass provides a counter‑theme to Jerry and earns specific praise for an inspiring solo during the jam sections.
band_member_comments.Bob: One reviewer felt Bob Weir’s playing was not particularly strong this night.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drummers (Bill ‘Pig’ and Mickey) are lauded for a stellar Alligator performance, fast‑and‑furious drum jams, and overall driving power.
